title: pouteria torta subsp. tuberculata
intro: Pouteria torta subsp. tuberculata, also known as the tuberculata or the white sipo, is a tree species found in the tropical regions of Central and South America, characterized by its smooth, gray bark and white sapwood. The tuberculata tree is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, derived from its wood and bark. The health benefits of this plant include its potential to reduce inflammation, alleviate respiratory issues, and treat various skin conditions. Traditionally, the tuberculata tree is prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or decoctions for its medicinal purposes, and its wood is also used for crafting and construction.
